Full Eventbrite integration planned\n\n---\n\n### FR8: Review & Rating Submission → Moderation Queue → Public Publish\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n\n#### Step 1: Verify Attendance\n```\nUser attempts to submit review\n  ↓\nGET /events/{event_id}/attendees/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nFind attendee by email\n  ↓\nGET /events/{event_id}/attendees/{attendee_id}/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nCheck: cancelled=false AND refunded=false?\n  ↓\nIf yes: attendance verified ✅\n```\n\n#### Step 2: Review Submission\n```\nIf attendance verified: Review record created\n  ↓\nStatus: SUBMITTED\n  ↓\nAdded to moderation queue\n```\n\n#### Step 3: Moderation\n```\nModeration engine runs:\n  - Profanity filter\n  - Spam detection (OpenAI moderation API)\n  ↓\nIf APPROVED: status → APPROVED\n  ↓\nIf REJECTED: status → REJECTED (terminal)\n```\n\n**Review States:** `SUBMITTED → PENDING_MODERATION → APPROVED → PUBLISHED / REJECTED`\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/attendees/` — list attendees\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/attendees/{attendee_id}/` — check attendance status\n\n**Constraints:** ✅ No constraints. No review write API exists on Eventbrite — reviews are 100% internal.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; Eventbrite read-only for attendance verification\n\n---\n\n### FR9: Admin Dashboard → Event CRUD → Seat Inventory Management\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n\n#### Event CRUD\n```\nAdmin creates/edits/deletes events in dashboard\n  ↓\nInternal event record updated\n  ↓\nPOST /organizations/{org_id}/events/ [Eventbrite] — create\n  ↓\nOR\nPOST /events/{event_id}/ [Eventbrite] — update\n  ↓\nOR\nPOST /events/{event_id}/cancel/ [Eventbrite] — cancel (triggers bulk refund)\n```\n\n#### Seat Inventory Management\n```\nAdmin manages ticket tiers, capacities, seat maps\n  ↓\nInternal seat_maps and pricing_tiers updated\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/capacity_tier/ [Eventbrite]\n```\n\n#### Venue & Media Management\n```\nAdmin uploads event images, banners\n  ↓\nPOST /media/upload/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nAdmin updates venue details\n  ↓\nPUT /venues/{venue_id}/ [Eventbrite]\n```\n\n#### Event Description & Content\n```\nAdmin writes rich HTML description\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/structured_content/{version}/ [Eventbrite]\n```\n\n#### Reporting & Analytics\n```\nAdmin views sales and attendance reports\n  ↓\nGET /reports/sales/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nGET /events/{event_id}/attendees/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nMerge with internal analytics\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `POST /organizations/{org_id}/events/` — create event\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/` — update event\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/cancel/` — cancel event\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/` — create ticket tier\n- `PUT /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/{class_id}/` — update tier\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/capacity_tier/` — update capacity\n- `POST /media/upload/` — upload images\n- `PUT /venues/{venue_id}/` — update venue\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/structured_content/{version}/` — set description\n- `GET /reports/sales/` — sales report\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/attendees/` — attendee list\n\n**Constraints:** ✅ No major constraints.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; Full admin CRUD planned\n\n---\n\n### FR10: Seat Lock State Machine with Conflict Resolution\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n\n#### State Transitions\n```\nAVAILABLE\n  ↓ [SELECT] + AvailabilityGuard\n  ↓ (check internal DB + Eventbrite ticket_classes count)\n  ↓\nSOFT_LOCKED  (held for cart session TTL via Redis)\n  ↓ [CONFIRM] + CartTtlGuard\n  ↓ (user proceeds to checkout)\n  ↓\nHARD_LOCKED  (held during Eventbrite checkout)\n  ↓ [PAY]\n  ↓\nPAYMENT_PENDING\n  ↓ [CONFIRM_PAYMENT] (onOrderComplete callback received)\n  ↓\nCONFIRMED  (eventbrite_order_id stored in DB)\n\nOR any state → [RELEASE] → AVAILABLE\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Checks:**\n- At AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED transition:\n  - `GET /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/for_sale/` — remaining count > 0?\n  - `GET /events/{event_id}/inventory_tiers/{id}/` — tier capacity available?\n\n**Conflict Resolution:**\n```\nIf Redis lock fails (seat already taken):\n  ↓\nConflictResolution triggered\n  ↓\nUser informed: \"Seat is now unavailable\"\n  ↓\nSeat returned to AVAILABLE state for next user\n```\n\n**Rollback (Payment Failure):**\n```\nUser's Eventbrite checkout fails\n  ↓\nonOrderComplete callback NOT fired\n  ↓\nCartTtlGuard releases seat lock \n  ↓\nSeat → AVAILABLE for next user\n```\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **No seat lock API on Eventbrite.** The entire state machine is internal. Seats are locked via Redis, conflict resolution is internal, and rollback is triggered by timer expiration. Eventbrite is consulted only for availability validation.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Locking is 100% internal\n\n---\n\n## 4. What Eventbrite Cannot Do — Implemented Internally\n\n| Gap | Handled Internally |\n|---|---|\n| User creation/sync | No user write API. Identity is 100% internal. Users linked post-purchase via order email. |\n| Conflict validation | No conflict API. Time slot conflicts and turnaround gaps enforced in internal DB. |\n| Seat locking | No seat lock API. State machine (Redis + Spring State Machine) is internal. |\n| Cart management | No cart API. Cart assembly and group discount rules are internal. |\n| Order creation | No order creation API. Orders created exclusively by Eventbrite Checkout Widget. |\n| Single order cancel | No per-order cancel API. Only full event cancel exists. Per-order cancellation not achievable. |\n| Refund submission | No programmatic refund API. Refunds triggered by mimicking admin actions. |\n| Reviews | No reviews API. Entire system is internal. Eventbrite only verifies attendance. |\n| Public event search | Deprecated API. Event discovery limited to org events only. |\n\n---\n\n## 5. Critical Integration Constraints Summary\n\n### ✅ Fully Achievable\n- Event creation, update, publish, unpublish, cancel\n- Ticket tier creation and management\n- Venue creation and management\n- User profile linking (post-purchase only)\n- Attendance verification for reviews\n- Sales reporting and attendee lists\n- Discount code creation and tracking\n- Recurring event schedules\n- Capacity tier management\n\n### ⚠️ Partially Achievable (Workarounds Required)\n- **Single-order cancellation** — No API exists. Option: Mimic admin action via org token (not officially supported).\n- **Programmatic refunds** — No API exists. Status is read-only. Refunds triggered by mimicking admin actions.\n- **Turnaround policy enforcement** — Internal DB logic only. Eventbrite has no conflict check API.\n\n### ❌ Not Achievable\n- **Public event search** — Deprecated API. Limited to org events.\n- **Direct seat locking** — Must use internal Redis + Spring State Machine.\n- **Cart persistence on Eventbrite** — Cart is 100% internal.\n- **User creation on Eventbrite** — No write API. Users are internal only.\n- **Review storage on Eventbrite** — No reviews API. System is internal.\n- **Order creation via API** — Only Checkout Widget can create orders.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Webhook Integration\n\n### Webhook Types Registered\n\n**Event Webhooks:**\n- `event.updated` — Admin updates event details\n- `event.published` — Event goes live\n- `event.unpublished` — Event comes offline\n- `event.canceled` — Event cancelled\n\n**Order Webhooks:**\n- `order.placed` — Order created (parallel to frontend callback)\n- `order.refunded` — Refund processed\n- `attendee.checked_in` — Attendee checked in at venue\n\n### Webhook Flow\n```\nEventbrite fires webhook event\n  ↓\nEbWebhookController receives payload\n  ↓\nEbWebhookDispatcher routes to appropriate listener\n  ↓\nInternal Spring Event published\n  ↓\nModules listen and react asynchronously\n```\n\n**Example:**\n```\norder.placed webhook received\n  ↓\nOrderCompletedEvent published internally\n  ↓\npayments-ticketing listens and confirms booking\n  ↓\nengagement listens and unlocks review eligibility\n  ↓\n[Happens in parallel to frontend callback]\n```\n\n**Note:** Modules NEVER consume raw webhooks directly. All webhooks are translated to internal Spring Events.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Summary Table: Eventbrite Integration Coverage\n\n| FR | Flow | Status | Eventbrite Calls | Constraints |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| FR1 | City → Venue → Events | ✅ Designed | `GET /orgs/events`, `GET /orgs/venues` | Limited to org events (public search deprecated) |\n| FR2 | Show Scheduling | ✅ Designed | `POST /events`, `POST /ticket_classes`, `POST /schedules`, `POST /publish` | Turnaround gaps internal-only |\n| FR3 | User Registration | ✅ Designed | (none at registration) | Zero Eventbrite integration; post-purchase only |\n| FR4 | Seat Selection | ✅ Designed | `GET /ticket_classes`, `GET /inventory_tiers` | No seat lock API; locking is internal |\n| FR5 | Payment & Ticketing | ✅ Designed | `GET /orders`, `GET /attendees`, JS SDK checkout widget | No order creation API; widget only |\n| FR6 | Cancellation | ⚠️ Partial | `GET /orders`, `POST /events/cancel` | No single-order cancel; no refund API |\n| FR7 | Coupons | ✅ Designed | `POST /discounts`, `GET /discounts`, `DELETE /discounts` | (none) |\n| FR8 | Reviews | ✅ Designed | `GET /attendees` (verify only) | No reviews API; system is internal |\n| FR9 | Admin Dashboard | ✅ Designed | Full CRUD: post/put/delete events, venues, tickets | (none) |\n| FR10 | Seat Lock State Machine | ✅ Designed | One-time availability check | Lock/conflict resolution is internal |\n\n---\n\n## 8. Architecture Diagram\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│        Your Spring Boot App              │\n│  (Internal DB = Source of Truth)         │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────┘\n           ↓           ↓           ↓\n    [Sync]      [Read]      [Write]\n      ↓           ↓           ↓\n┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│     shared/eventbrite/service/           │\n│  ACL Facades (Only Integration Point)    │\n└──────────────────────────────────────────┘\n      ↓                           ↓\n  [REST API]               [Webhooks]\n      ↓                           ↓\n┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│         Eventbrite API v3                │\n│  Ticketing | Payment | Catalog | Orders  │\n└──────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n---\n\n## 9. Recommendations Going Forward\n\n### High Priority (Unblock FR6 Cancellation)\n1. **Single-Order Cancel Workaround** — Investigate org-token-based cancellation mimicking admin action. Document the undocumented API endpoint.\n2. **Refund Status Polling** — Implement robust polling mechanism to track refund status post-cancellation.\n\n### Medium Priority (Improve Robustness)\n1. **Webhook Retry Logic** — Add exponential backoff for failed webhook deliveries.\n2. **Orphan Detection Job** — Implement background job to flag and reconcile orphaned `eventbrite_event_id` records.\n3. **Rate Limiting** — Add circuit breaker pattern for Eventbrite API calls to handle rate limits.\n\n### Low Priority (Nice-to-Have)\n1. **Event Analytics Dashboard** — Pull sales and attendance reports from Eventbrite API.\n2. **Multi-Org Support** — Extend ACL facades to support multiple Eventbrite organizations.\n\n---\n\n## 10. References\n\n- Eventbrite API v3 Docs: `docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib`\n- Module ownership: `PRODUCT.md`\n- Architecture rules: `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md`\n- Coding standards: `agents/CODING_AGENT.md`\n"
```json
  }
}
```

## Entry 31

**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T04:13:32.822Z

**Role/Type:** response_item

**Content:**

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T04:13:32.822Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_UIgNedMUemJAlQMNQuPBtVMq",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 7b44dd\nWall time: 0.0517 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3366\nOutput:\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\n\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\n> in this codebase. Every agent, developer, or tool that adds code MUST follow this structure\n> exactly. It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\n\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\n```\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\n    ↓\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\n    ↓\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\n    ↓\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\n    ↓\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\n    ↓\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\n```\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\n\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\n|---|---|---|\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\n\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\nvia the ACL facade services.\n\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\n\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\n\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\n\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\n\n---\n\n## Project Layout\n\n```\n[project-root]/\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\n```\n\n---\n\n## shared/ Structure\n\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\n\n```\nshared/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\n    │\n    ├── security/\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\n    │\n    ├── common/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\n    │   ├── dto/\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\n    │   └── exception/\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\n    │\n    └── config/\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\n```\n\n---\n\n## [module-name]/ Structure\n\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    ├── api/\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\n    │   └── dto/\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\n    │\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\n    │\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\n    │\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\n    │\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\n    │\n    ├── event/\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\n    │\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\n    │   ├── guard/\n    │   └── action/\n    │\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\n```\n\n---\n\n## admin/ and app/\n\n```\nadmin/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\n\napp/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\n    └── resources/\n        ├── application.yml\n        ├── application-local.yml\n        ├── application-prod.yml\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\n\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\n|---|---|---|\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\n| Using `@CrossOrigin` on individual controllers | Scattered CORS config — inconsistent across modules | Define CORS once in `shared/config/WebConfig` |\n\n---\n\n## Event Naming Conventions\n\n```\nPublished events  →  Past tense + \"Event\" suffix\n                     [WhatHappened]Event\n                     e.g. BookingConfirmedEvent\n                          SeatReleasedEvent\n                          UserRegisteredEvent\n                          PaymentFailedEvent\n\nListeners         →  [TriggerEvent]Listener\n                     e.g. PaymentFailedListener (inside booking-inventory module)\n                          BookingConfirmedListener (inside engagement module)\n                          CartAssembledListener (inside payments module)\n\nEvent payload     →  Only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects.\n                     NEVER include @Entity objects in events.\n                     NEVER include mutable collections.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Module pom.xml Dependency Rules\n\n```xml\n<!-- Every module pom.xml follows this exact pattern -->\n<dependencies>\n\n  <!-- 1. Always: shared internal library -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>shared</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 2. Only upstream modules (modules this one reads from) -->\n  <!-- Example: booking-inventory reads from discovery-catalog -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>[upstream-module]</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 3. NEVER depend on a peer or downstream module -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on admin/ -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on app/ -->\n\n</dependencies>\n```\n\n---\n\n## Flyway Migration Naming\n\n```\nV[auto-increment]__[verb]_[table_name].sql\n\nExamples:\n  V1__create_users.sql\n  V2__create_events.sql\n  V7__add_wallet_balance_to_users.sql\n  V12__create_seat_lock_audit_log.sql\n\nRules:\n  - NEVER modify an existing migration file (Flyway will reject it in prod)\n  - ALWAYS add a new file for any schema change\n  - Version numbers are global across ALL modules — maintain a single sequence in app/\n  - Table names use snake_case\n  - Description uses snake_case with underscores\n```\n"
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_UIgNedMUemJAlQMNQuPBtVMq",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 7b44dd\nWall time: 0.0517 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3366\nOutput:\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\n\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\n> in this codebase. Every agent, developer, or tool that adds code MUST follow this structure\n> exactly. It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\n\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\n```\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\n    ↓\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\n    ↓\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\n    ↓\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\n    ↓\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\n    ↓\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\n```\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\n\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\n|---|---|---|\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\n\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\nvia the ACL facade services.\n\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\n\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\n\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\n\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\n\n---\n\n## Project Layout\n\n```\n[project-root]/\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\n```\n\n---\n\n## shared/ Structure\n\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\n\n```\nshared/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\n    │\n    ├── security/\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\n    │\n    ├── common/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\n    │   ├── dto/\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\n    │   └── exception/\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\n    │\n    └── config/\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\n```\n\n---\n\n## [module-name]/ Structure\n\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    ├── api/\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\n    │   └── dto/\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\n    │\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\n    │\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\n    │\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\n    │\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\n    │\n    ├── event/\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\n    │\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\n    │   ├── guard/\n    │   └── action/\n    │\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\n```\n\n---\n\n## admin/ and app/\n\n```\nadmin/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\n\napp/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\n    └── resources/\n        ├── application.yml\n        ├── application-local.yml\n        ├── application-prod.yml\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\n\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\n|---|---|---|\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\n| Using `@CrossOrigin` on individual controllers | Scattered CORS config — inconsistent across modules | Define CORS once in `shared/config/WebConfig` |\n\n---\n\n## Event Naming Conventions\n\n```\nPublished events  →  Past tense + \"Event\" suffix\n                     [WhatHappened]Event\n                     e.g. BookingConfirmedEvent\n                          SeatReleasedEvent\n                          UserRegisteredEvent\n                          PaymentFailedEvent\n\nListeners         →  [TriggerEvent]Listener\n                     e.g. PaymentFailedListener (inside booking-inventory module)\n                          BookingConfirmedListener (inside engagement module)\n                          CartAssembledListener (inside payments module)\n\nEvent payload     →  Only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects.\n                     NEVER include @Entity objects in events.\n                     NEVER include mutable collections.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Module pom.xml Dependency Rules\n\n```xml\n<!-- Every module pom.xml follows this exact pattern -->\n<dependencies>\n\n  <!-- 1. Always: shared internal library -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>shared</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 2. Only upstream modules (modules this one reads from) -->\n  <!-- Example: booking-inventory reads from discovery-catalog -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>[upstream-module]</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 3. NEVER depend on a peer or downstream module -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on admin/ -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on app/ -->\n\n</dependencies>\n```\n\n---\n\n## Flyway Migration Naming\n\n```\nV[auto-increment]__[verb]_[table_name].sql\n\nExamples:\n  V1__create_users.sql\n  V2__create_events.sql\n  V7__add_wallet_balance_to_users.sql\n  V12__create_seat_lock_audit_log.sql\n\nRules:\n  - NEVER modify an existing migration file (Flyway will reject it in prod)\n  - ALWAYS add a new file for any schema change\n  - Version numbers are global across ALL modules — maintain a single sequence in app/\n  - Table names use snake_case\n  - Description uses snake_case with underscores\n```\n"
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T04:13:36.164Z",

  "payload": {

    "call_id": "call_HODiLfrV3FeMxXZADrwajIcc",
    "output": "Chunk ID: a46ae4\nWall time: 0.0511 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1166\nOutput:\n# TEST_AGENT.md — Testing Standards & Execution Agent\n\n> Read this file before writing or running tests.\n> This agent is responsible for test planning, test implementation, execution, failure fixing, and result logging.\n\n---\n\n## Purpose\n\nThis file defines the responsibilities of the **Test Agent**.\n\n- Build tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8 test list (source of truth for test scope)\n- Follow project testing architecture in `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md`\n- Execute tests and fix failures caused by new or changed code\n- Keep production code and test code aligned with module boundaries from `PRODUCT.md`\n- Produce a timestamped test result log in `test_result_folder/` at the repo root\n\n---\n\n## Scope Ownership\n\n### Test Agent MUST\n\n- Write tests for all layers listed in spec:\n  - `domain/` (pure unit)\n  - `service/` (Mockito orchestration)\n  - `api/` (`@WebMvcTest` HTTP contract)\n  - `arch/` (ArchUnit boundaries)\n- Run relevant tests after implementation\n- Fix failing tests and related test setup issues\n- Fix production code only when required to satisfy approved spec behavior\n- Log all found errors and all applied fixes in a test report file\n\n### Test Agent MUST NOT\n\n- Introduce unapproved features not present in `SPEC_*.md`\n- Change module ownership or dependency direction rules\n- Add module-level `@ControllerAdvice`\n- Skip failed tests without documenting reason and impact\n\n---\n\n## Inputs (Required Before Writing Tests)\n\n1. Confirmed feature spec: `SPEC_*.md`\n2. Module ownership and boundaries: `PRODUCT.md`\n3. Layer/package standards: `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md`\n4. Test patterns/rules: `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md`\n5. Coding constraints for compatibility: `agents/CODING_AGENT.md`\n\n---\n\n## Test Design Standards\n\n### AAA Format (Mandatory)\n\nAll tests must follow Arrange–Act–Assert.\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_400_when_cityId_missing() {\n    // Arrange\n    MockHttpServletRequestBuilder request = get(\"/api/v1/catalog/venues\");\n\n    // Act\n    ResultActions result = mockMvc.perform(request);\n\n    // Assert\n    result.andExpect(status().isBadRequest());\n}\n```\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`should_[expected_behaviour]_when_[condition]`\n\nExamples:\n- `should_soft_delete_event_when_missing_from_org_sync`\n- `should_return_401_when_webhook_signature_invalid`\n- `should_not_query_db_when_cache_hit`\n\n### Layer Rules\n\n- `domain/`: no Spring context, no mocks for infra\n- `service/`: Mockito only (`@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)`)\n- `api/`: `@WebMvcTest`, services/mappers mocked\n- `arch/`: ArchUnit boundary checks based on hard rules\n\n---\n\n## Execution Workflow\n\n1. Parse Stage 8 test list from the approved `SPEC_*.md`\n2. Generate/adjust tests by layer\n3. Run focused tests first (changed files only), then module suite\n4. Capture failures\n5. Fix test or code issues\n6. Re-run until green (or blocked)\n7. Produce and save test report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Failure Triage Rules\n\nWhen tests fail, classify and resolve in this order:\n\n1. **Test setup issue** (mock, fixture, context) → fix test\n2. **Contract mismatch** (spec vs endpoint/DTO) → fix code or test to match approved spec\n3. **Behavior bug** (business logic wrong) → fix production code\n4. **Architecture violation** (ArchUnit) → fix package/dependency layering\n5. **External/infra flake** → document as blocked with reproducible evidence\n\nDo not silently ignore failing tests.\n\n---\n\n## Required Result Logging\n\nAfter every test cycle, generate a result log file in:\n\n`test_result_folder/`\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`[feature_or_flow]_[yyyyMMdd_HHmmss].md`\n\nExamples:\n- `fr1_catalog_search_20260303_201530.md`\n- `payment_confirmation_flow_20260303_204012.md`\n\n### Log Template (Mandatory)\n\n```markdown\n# Test Result — [Feature/Flow]\n\n- Timestamp: [yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss]\n- Spec: [SPEC_*.md]\n- Module(s): [module list]\n- Commit/Working State: [hash or local]\n\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\n- Total planned:\n- Implemented:\n- Passed:\n- Failed:\n- Skipped:\n\n## Failures Found\n1. [test name]\n   - Error:\n   - Root cause:\n   - Fix applied:\n\n## Code/Test Changes Applied\n- [file path] — [short change summary]\n\n## Final Status\n- [PASS | PARTIAL | BLOCKED]\n- Notes:\n```\n\n---\n\n## Completion Criteria\n\nTest Agent is complete only when:\n\n- All feasible Stage 8 tests are implemented\n- Relevant tests are executed\n- Failures are fixed or explicitly documented as blocked\n- A timestamped result log is saved in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Handoff Contract\n\n- `SPEC_GENERATOR` defines what to test\n- `CODING_AGENT` implements production code\n- `TEST_AGENT` validates behavior, fixes test/logic defects, and publishes the final test report\n"
  }
}
```

Fix test or code issues\n6. Re-run until green (or blocked)\n7. Produce and save test report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Failure Triage Rules\n\nWhen tests fail, classify and resolve in this order:\n\n1. **Test setup issue** (mock, fixture, context) → fix test\n2. **Contract mismatch** (spec vs endpoint/DTO) → fix code or test to match approved spec\n3. **Behavior bug** (business logic wrong) → fix production code\n4. **Architecture violation** (ArchUnit) → fix package/dependency layering\n5. **External/infra flake** → document as blocked with reproducible evidence\n\nDo not silently ignore failing tests.\n\n---\n\n## Required Result Logging\n\nAfter every test cycle, generate a result log file in:\n\n`test_result_folder/`\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`[feature_or_flow]_[yyyyMMdd_HHmmss].md`\n\nExamples:\n- `fr1_catalog_search_20260303_201530.md`\n- `payment_confirmation_flow_20260303_204012.md`\n\n### Log Template (Mandatory)\n\n```markdown\n# Test Result — [Feature/Flow]\n\n- Timestamp: [yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss]\n- Spec: [SPEC_*.md]\n- Module(s): [module list]\n- Commit/Working State: [hash or local]\n\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\n- Total planned:\n- Implemented:\n- Passed:\n- Failed:\n- Skipped:\n\n## Failures Found\n1. [test name]\n   - Error:\n   - Root cause:\n   - Fix applied:\n\n## Code/Test Changes Applied\n- [file path] — [short change summary]\n\n## Final Status\n- [PASS | PARTIAL | BLOCKED]\n- Notes:\n```\n\n---\n\n## Completion Criteria\n\nTest Agent is complete only when:\n\n- All feasible Stage 8 tests are implemented\n- Relevant tests are executed\n- Failures are fixed or explicitly documented as blocked\n- A timestamped result log is saved in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Handoff Contract\n\n- `SPEC_GENERATOR` defines what to test\n- `CODING_AGENT` implements production code\n- `TEST_AGENT` validates behavior, fixes test/logic defects, and publishes the final test report\n"
```json
  }
}
```
