page_size, page)\n    return {\"pagination\": pagination.model_dump(), \"events\": [_event_to_response(e).model_dump() for e in items]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/series/{event_series_id}/\", response_model=EventResponse, dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef get_series_parent(event_series_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):\n    event = _ensure_event(db, event_series_id)\n    if not event.is_series:\n        raise ApiError(\"NOT_FOUND\", \"Invalid series id.\", 404)\n    return _event_to_response(event)\n\n\n@router.get(\"/events/search/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef search_events(\n    q: Optional[str] = Query(default=None),\n    page_size: int = Query(default=50),\n    page: int = Query(default=1),\n    db: Session = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    events = db.query(Event).all()\n    if q:\n        events = [e for e in events if q.lower() in e.name.get(\"text\", \"\").lower()]\n    items, pagination = paginate(events, page_size, page)\n    return {\"pagination\": pagination.model_dump(), \"events\": [_event_to_response(e).model_dump() for e in items]}\n"

      end={\"timezone\": \"UTC\", \"utc\": payload.end.utc, \"local\": payload.end.utc.replace(\"Z\", \"\")},\n        created=now,\n        changed=now,\n        published=None,\n        status=\"draft\",\n        currency=payload.currency,\n        online_event=payload.online_event or False,\n        listed=payload.listed if payload.listed is not None else True,\n        shareable=payload.shareable if payload.shareable is not None else True,\n        invite_only=payload.invite_only if payload.invite_only is not None else False,\n        show_remaining=payload.show_remaining if payload.show_remaining is not None else False,\n        password=payload.password,\n        capacity=payload.capacity,\n dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef update_event(event_id: str, body: dict, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):\n    if \"event\" not in body:\n        raise ApiError(\"ARGUMENTS_ERROR\", \"Missing event object.\", 400)\n    payload = EventUpdate(**body[\"event\"])\n    _validate_event_update(payload)\n\n    event = _ensure_event(db, event_id)\n\n    if payload.name:\n        event.name = {\"text\": payload.name.html, \"html\": payload.name.html}\n    if payload.summary is not None:\n        event.summary = payload.summary\n    if payload.description is not None:\n        event.description = payload.description.model_dump()\n    if payload.start:\n        event.start = {\"timezone\": \"UTC\", \"utc\": payload.start.utc, \"local\": payload.start.utc.replace(\"Z\", \"\")}\n    if payload.end:\n        event.end = {\"timezone\": \"UTC\", \"utc\": payload.end.utc, \"local\": payload.end.utc.replace(\"Z\", \"\")}\n    if payload.venue_id is not None:\n        event.venue_id = payload.venue_id\n    if payload.currency is not None:\n        event.currency = payload.currency\n    if payload.online_event is not None:\n        event.online_event = payload.online_event\n    if payload.listed is not None:\n        event.listed = payload.listed\n    if payload.shareable is not None:\n        event.shareable = payload.shareable\n    if payload.invite_only is not None:\n        event.invite_only = payload.invite_only\n    if payload.show_remaining is not None:\n        event.show_remaining = payload.show_remaining\n    if payload.password is not None:\n        event.password = payload.password\n    if payload.capacity is not None:\n        event.capacity = payload.capacity\n        event.capacity_is_custom = True\n    if payload.is_series is not None:\n        event.is_series = payload.is_series\n    if payload.is_reserved_seating is not None:\n        event.is_reserved_seating = payload.is_reserved_seating\n    if payload.show_pick_a_seat is not None:\n        event.show_pick_a_seat = payload.show_pick_a_seat\n    if payload.show_seatmap_thumbnail is not None:\n        event.show_seatmap_thumbnail = payload.show_seatmap_thumbnail\n    if payload.show_colors_in_seatmap_thumbnail is not None:\n        event.show_colors_in_seatmap_thumbnail = payload.show_colors_in_seatmap_thumbnail\n    if payload.source is not None:\n        event.source = payload.source\n\n    event.changed = _now_iso()\n    db.commit()\n    db.refresh(event)\n    return _event_to_response(event)\n\n\n@router.delete(\"/events/{event_id}/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef delete_event(event_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):\n    event = _ensure_event(db, event_id)\n    if event.status != \"draft\":\n        raise ApiError(\"CANNOT_DELETE\", \"You cannot delete an event that has pending or completed orders.\", 400)\n    db.delete(event)\n    db.commit()\n    return {\"deleted\": True}\n\n\n@router.post(\"/events/{event_id}/publish/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\nasync def publish_event(event_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):\n    event = _ensure_event(db, event_id)\n    if event.status == \"live\":\n        raise ApiError(\"ALREADY_PUBLISHED_OR_DELETED\", \"This event has already been published or deleted.\", 400)\n    event.status = \"live\"\n    event.published = _now_iso()\n    event.changed = _now_iso()\n    db.commit()\n    await dispatch_webhook(db, \"event.published\", f\"https://www.eventbriteapi.com/v3/events/{event.id}/\", _event_to_response(event).model_dump())\n    return {\"published\": True}\n\n\n@router.post(\"/events/{event_id}/unpublish/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\nasync def unpublish_event(event_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):\n    event = _ensure_event(db, event_id)\n    if event.status != \"live\":\n        raise ApiError(\"NOT_PUBLISHED\", \"This event is not currently published and cannot be unpublished.\", 400)\n    event.status = \"draft\"\n    event.changed = _now_iso()\n    db.commit()\n    await dispatch_webhook(db, \"event.unpublished\", f\"https://www.eventbriteapi.com/v3/events/{event.id}/\", _event_to_response(event).model_dump())\n    return {\"unpublished\": True}\n\n\n@router.post(\"/events/{event_id}/cancel/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\nasync def cancel_event(event_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):\n    event = _ensure_event(db, event_id)\n    if event.status == \"canceled\":\n        raise ApiError(\"ALREADY_CANCELED\", \"This event has already been canceled.\", 400)\n    event.status = \"canceled\"\n    event.changed = _now_iso()\n    db.commit()\n    await dispatch_webhook(db, \"event.canceled\", f\"https://www.eventbriteapi.com/v3/events/{event.id}/\", _event_to_response(event).model_dump())\n    return {\"canceled\": True}\n\n\n@router.post(\"/events/{event_id}/copy/\", response_model=EventResponse, dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef copy_event(event_id: str, body: dict, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):\n    event = _ensure_event(db, event_id)\n    payload = EventCopy(**body) if body else EventCopy()\n\n    new_id = f\"event_{int(datetime.now().timestamp())}_copy\"\n    now = _now_iso()\n    start = event.start\n    end = event.end\n    if payload.start_date:\n        start = {\"timezone\": payload.timezone or \"UTC\", \"utc\": payload.start_date, \"local\": payload.start_date.replace(\"Z\", \"\")}\n    if payload.end_date:\n        end = {\"timezone\": payload.timezone or \"UTC\", \"utc\": payload.end_date, \"local\": payload.end_date.replace(\"Z\", \"\")}\n\n    new_event = Event(\n        id=new_id,\n        organization_id=event.organization_id,\n        venue_id=event.venue_id,\n        name={\"text\": payload.name or event.name.get(\"text\", \"\"), \"html\": payload.name or event.name.get(\"html\", \"\")},\n        summary=payload.summary or event.summary,\n        description=event.description,\n        start=start,\n        end=end,\n capacity_is_custom=event.capacity_is_custom,\n        is_series=event.is_series,\n        series_parent_id=event.series_parent_id,\n        is_reserved_seating=event.is_reserved_seating,\n        show_pick_a_seat=event.show_pick_a_seat,\n        show_seatmap_thumbnail=event.show_seatmap_thumbnail,\n        show_colors_in_seatmap_thumbnail=event.show_colors_in_seatmap_thumbnail,\n        organizer_id=event.organizer_id,\n        logo_id=event.logo_id,\n        format_id=event.format_id,\n        category_id=event.category_id,\n        subcategory_id=event.subcategory_id,\n        locale=event.locale,\n        source=event.source,\n    )\n    db.add(new_event)\n    db.commit()\n    db.refresh(new_event)\n    return _event_to_response(new_event)\n\n\n@router.get(\"/venues/{venue_id}/events/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef list_events_by_venue(\n    venue_id: str,\n    status: Optional[str] = Query(default=None),\n    page_size: int = Query(default=50),\n    page: int = Query(default=1),\n    db: Session = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    events = db.query(Event).filter(Event.venue_id == venue_id).all()\n    if status and status != \"all\":\n        status_set = {s.strip() for s in status.split(\",\")}\n        events = [e for e in events if e.status in status_set]\n    items, pagination = paginate(events, page_size, page)\n    return {\"pagination\": pagination.model_dump(), \"events\": [_event_to_response(e).model_dump() for e in items]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/organizations/{organization_id}/events/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef list_events_by_org(\n    organization_id: str,\n    status: Optional[str] = Query(default=None),\n    page_size: int = Query(default=50),\n    page: int = Query(default=1),\n    db: Session = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    events = db.query(Event).filter(Event.organization_id == organization_id).all()\n    if status and status != \"all\":\n        status_set = {s.strip() for s in status.split(\",\")}\n        events = [e for e in events if e.status in status_set]\n    items, pagination = paginate(events, page_size, page)\n    return {\"pagination\": pagination.model_dump(), \"events\": [_event_to_response(e).model_dump() for e in items]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/series/{event_series_id}/events/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef list_events_by_series(\n    event_series_id: str,\n    page_size: int = Query(default=50),\n    page: int = Query(default=1),\n    db: Session = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    events = db.query(Event).filter(Event.series_parent_id == event_series_id).all()\n    items, pagination = paginate(events, page_size, page)\n    return {\"pagination\": pagination.model_dump(), \"events\": [_event_to_response(e).model_dump() for e in items]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/series/{event_series_id}/\", response_model=EventResponse, dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef get_series_parent(event_series_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):\n    event = _ensure_event(db, event_series_id)\n    if not event.is_series:\n        raise ApiError(\"NOT_FOUND\", \"Invalid series id.\", 404)\n    return _event_to_response(event)\n\n\n@router.get(\"/events/search/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef search_events(\n    q: Optional[str] = Query(default=None),\n    page_size: int = Query(default=50),\n    page: int = Query(default=1),\n    db: Session = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    events = db.query(Event).all()\n    if q:\n        events = [e for e in events if q.lower() in e.name.get(\"text\", \"\").lower()]\n    items, pagination = paginate(events, page_size, page)\n    return {\"pagination\": pagination.model_dump(), \"events\": [_event_to_response(e).model_dump() for e in items]}\n"

    "output": "Chunk ID: c841c8\nWall time: 0.0511 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 241\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, DeclarativeBase\nfrom sqlalchemy.pool import StaticPool\n\nfrom app.config import settings\n\n\nclass Base(DeclarativeBase):\n    pass\n\n\ndef _make_engine():\n    if settings.database_url.startswith(\"sqlite\") and \":memory:\" in settings.database_url:\n        return create_engine(\n            settings.database_url,\n            connect_args={\"check_same_thread\": False},\n            poolclass=StaticPool,\n            future=True,\n        )\n    return create_engine(settings.database_url, future=True)\n\n\nengine = _make_engine()\nSessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine, autoflush=False, autocommit=False, future=True)\n\n\ndef init_db():\n    from app import models  # noqa: F401\n\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n\n\ndef reset_db():\n    from app import models  # noqa: F401\n\n    Base.metadata.drop_all(bind=engine)\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n"
  }
}

{

  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Planning test client setup with httpx and startup handling**\n\nI'm opting to create tests using httpx.Client with ASGITransport to avoid adding requests as a dependency and to ensure startup events run properly by enabling lifespan handling. Next, I'll build a test client fixture that can set auth headers while being mindful of the RateLimitMiddleware possibly depending on client IP logic."
  }
}
```

## Entry 101

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T04:14:22.790Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_st2X22hW3XK6soTqCkca7tuU",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 3365ef\nWall time: 0.0509 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 429\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom fastapi import Request\nfrom starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware\n\nfrom app.services.rate_limiter import rate_limiter\nfrom app.exception.exceptions import ApiError\n\n\nEVENT_ACTION_PREFIXES = {\n    \"POST:/v3/organizations/\",  # create event\n    \"POST:/v3/events/\",  # update or publish/cancel/copy/schedules\n}\n\n\nclass RateLimitMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):\n    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next):\n        token = request.headers.get(\"Authorization\", \"\").replace(\"Bearer\", \"\").strip()\n        if not token:\n            token = request.query_params.get(\"token\", \"\") or \"\"\n\n        if token:\n            is_event_action = _is_event_action(request)\n            usage = rate_limiter.check_and_increment(token, is_event_action)\n            response = await call_next(request)\n            _add_headers(response, usage)\n            return response\n\n        return await call_next(request)\n\n\ndef _is_event_action(request: Request) -> bool:\n    key = f\"{request.method}:{request.url.path}\"\n    if key.startswith(\"POST:/v3/organizations/\") and key.endswith(\"/events/\"):\n        return True\n    if key.startswith(\"POST:/v3/events/\") and (\n        key.endswith(\"/publish/\")\n        or key.endswith(\"/unpublish/\")\n        or key.endswith(\"/cancel/\")\n        or key.endswith(\"/copy/\")\n        or key.endswith(\"/schedules/\")\n    ):\n        return True\n    return False\n\n\ndef _add_headers(response, usage):\n    response.headers[\"X-RateLimit-Limit\"] = str(usage[\"hourly\"].limit)\n    response.headers[\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\"] = str(max(usage[\"hourly\"].limit - usage[\"hourly\"].usage, 0))\n    response.headers[\"X-RateLimit-Reset\"] = str(usage[\"hourly\"].reset_at)\n"
  }
}
```

import Order\nfrom app.schemas.order import OrderResponse\nfrom app.services.pagination import paginate\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/v3\", tags=[\"orders\"])\n\n\ndef _to_response(order: Order) -> OrderResponse:\n    return OrderResponse(\n        id=order.id,\n        created=order.created,\n        changed=order.changed,\n        name=order.name,\n        first_name=order.first_name,\n        last_name=order.last_name,\n        email=order.email,\n        costs=order.costs,\n        event_id=order.event_id,\n        time_remaining=int(order.time_remaining) if order.time_remaining else None,\n        questions=order.questions,\n        answers=order.answers,\n        promo_code=order.promo_code,\n        status=order.status,\n        refund_request=order.refund_request,\n    )\n\n\n@router.get(\"/orders/{order_id}/\", response_model=OrderResponse, dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef get_order(order_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):\n    order = db.get(Order, order_id)\n    if not order:\n        raise ApiError(\"NOT_FOUND\", \"Invalid URL.\", 404)\n    return _to_response(order)\n\n\n@router.get(\"/organizations/{organization_id}/orders/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef list_orders_by_org(\n    organization_id: str,\n    status: str | None = Query(default=None),\n    changed_since: str | None = Query(default=None),\n    only_emails: list[str] | None = Query(default=None),\n    exclude_emails: list[str] | None = Query(default=None),\n    page_size: int = Query(default=50),\n    page: int = Query(default=1),\n    db: Session = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    orders = db.query(Order).filter(Order.organization_id == organization_id).all()\n    if only_emails:\n        orders = [o for o in orders if o.email in only_emails]\n    if exclude_emails:\n        orders = [o for o in orders if o.email not in exclude_emails]\n    if status and status != \"both\":\n        orders = [o for o in orders if o.status == status]\n    items, pagination = paginate(orders, page_size, page)\n    return {\"pagination\": pagination.model_dump(), \"orders\": [_to_response(o).model_dump() for o in items]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/events/{event_id}/orders/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef list_orders_by_event(\n    event_id: str,\n    status: str | None = Query(default=None),\n    refund_request_statuses: list[str] | None = Query(default=None),\n    page_size: int = Query(default=50),\n    page: int = Query(default=1),\n    db: Session = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    orders = db.query(Order).filter(Order.event_id == event_id).all()\n    if status and status != \"both\":\n        orders = [o for o in orders if o.status == status]\n    if refund_request_statuses:\n        orders = [o for o in orders if o.refund_request and o.refund_request.get(\"status\") in refund_request_statuses]\n    items, pagination = paginate(orders, page_size, page)\n    return {\"pagination\": pagination.model_dump(), \"orders\": [_to_response(o).model_dump() for o in items]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/users/{user_id}/orders/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef list_orders_by_user(\n    user_id: str,\n    page_size: int = Query(default=50),\n    page: int = Query(default=1),\n    db: Session = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    # No user model; return all orders as mock\n    orders = db.query(Order).all()\n    items, pagination = paginate(orders, page_size, page)\n    return {\"pagination\": pagination.model_dump(), \"orders\": [_to_response(o).model_dump() for o in items]}\n"
  }
}

tags=[\"attendees\"])\n\n\ndef _to_response(att: Attendee) -> AttendeeResponse:\n    return AttendeeResponse(\n        id=att.id,\n        created=att.created,\n        changed=att.changed,\n profile=att.profile,\n        addresses=att.addresses,\n        questions=att.questions,\n        answers=att.answers,\n        barcodes=att.barcodes,\n        team=att.team,\n order_id=att.order_id,\n        guestlist_id=att.guestlist_id,\n        invited_by=att.invited_by,\n        delivery_method=att.delivery_method,\n    )\n\n\n@router.get(\"/events/{event_id}/attendees/{attendee_id}/\", response_model=AttendeeResponse, dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef get_attendee(event_id: str, attendee_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):\n    attendee = db.get(Attendee, attendee_id)\n    if not attendee or attendee.event_id != event_id:\n        raise ApiError(\"NOT_FOUND\", \"Invalid URL.\", 404)\n    return _to_response(attendee)\n\n\n@router.get(\"/events/{event_id}/attendees/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef list_attendees_by_event(\n    event_id: str,\n    status: str | None = Query(default=None),\n    page_size: int = Query(default=50),\n    page: int = Query(default=1),\n    db: Session = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    attendees = db.query(Attendee).filter(Attendee.event_id == event_id).all()\n    if status == \"attending\":\n        attendees = [a for a in attendees if a.status in (\"attending\", \"checked_in\")]\n    if status == \"not_attending\":\n        attendees = [a for a in attendees if a.status in (\"not_attending\", \"deleted\")]\n    items, pagination = paginate(attendees, page_size, page)\n    return {\"pagination\": pagination.model_dump(), \"attendees\": [_to_response(a).model_dump() for a in items]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/organizations/{organization_id}/attendees/\", dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])\ndef list_attendees_by_org(\n    organization_id: }

  }
}
```

## Entry 135

**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T04:15:13.544Z

**Role/Type:** response_item

**Content:**

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T04:15:13.544Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat <<'EOF' > mock-eventbrite-api/tests/conftest.py\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport httpx\\nimport pytest\\n\\nfrom app.main import create_app\\n\\n\\ndef auth_headers(token: str = \\\"test-token\\\") -> dict:\\n    return {\\\"Authorization\\\": f\\\"Bearer {token}\\\"}\\n\\n\\n@pytest.fixture()\\ndef client():\\n    app = create_app()\\n    transport = httpx.ASGITransport(app=app, lifespan=\\\"on\\\")\\n    with httpx.Client(transport=transport, base_url=\\\"http://test\\\") as client:\\n        reset = client.post(\\\"/mock/reset\\\")\\n        assert reset.status_code == 200\\n        yield client\\nEOF\",\"yield_time_ms\":1000}",

    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat <<'EOF' > mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_auth.py\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom tests.conftest import auth_headers\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_return_401_when_auth_is_missing(client):\\n    response = client.get(\\\"/v3/events/event_1/\\\")\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 401\\n    assert response.json() == {\\n        \\\"error\\\": \\\"NO_AUTH\\\",\\n        \\\"error_description\\\": \\\"Authentication not provided.\\\",\\n        \\\"status_code\\\": 401,\\n    }\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_return_400_when_auth_header_is_invalid(client):\\n    response = client.get(\\\"/v3/events/event_1/\\\", headers={\\\"Authorization\\\": \\\"Token abc\\\"})\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 400\\n    body = response.json()\\n    assert body[\\\"error\\\"] == \\\"INVALID_AUTH_HEADER\\\"\\n    assert body[\\\"status_code\\\"] == 400\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_allow_query_token_auth_and_attach_rate_limit_headers(client):\\n    response = client.get(\\\"/v3/events/event_1/?token=demo\\\")\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 200\\n    assert response.json()[\\\"id\\\"] == \\\"event_1\\\"\\n    assert \\\"X-RateLimit-Limit\\\" in response.headers\\n    assert \\\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\\\" in response.headers\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_allow_bearer_token_auth(client):\\n    response = client.get(\\\"/v3/events/event_1/\\\", headers=auth_headers())\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 200\\n    assert response.json()[\\\"id\\\"] == \\\"event_1\\\"\\nEOF\",\"yield_time_ms\":1000}",
    "call_id": "call_BIFszDVVbCIkzW7RervEEOUh"
  }
}
```

    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat <<'EOF' > mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_events.py\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom tests.conftest import auth_headers\\n\\n\\ndef _base_event_payload():\\n    return {\\n        \\\"event\\\": {\\n            \\\"name\\\": {\\\"html\\\": \\\"Test Event\\\"},\\n            \\\"start\\\": {\\\"timezone\\\": \\\"UTC\\\", \\\"utc\\\": \\\"2030-01-01T10:00:00Z\\\"},\\n            \\\"end\\\": {\\\"timezone\\\": \\\"UTC\\\", \\\"utc\\\": \\\"2030-01-01T12:00:00Z\\\"},\\n            \\\"currency\\\": \\\"USD\\\",\\n            \\\"venue_id\\\": \\\"venue_1\\\",\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_reject_event_with_venue_and_online_flag(client):\\n    payload = _base_event_payload()\\n    payload[\\\"event\\\"][\\\"online_event\\\"] = True\\n\\n    response = client.post(\\\"/v3/organizations/org_1/events/\\\", headers=auth_headers(), json=payload)\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 400\\n    body = response.json()\\n    assert body[\\\"error\\\"] == \\\"VENUE_AND_ONLINE\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_create_and_delete_draft_event(client):\\n    response = client.post(\\\"/v3/organizations/org_1/events/\\\", headers=auth_headers(), json=_base_event_payload())\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 200\\n    event_id = response.json()[\\\"id\\\"]\\n\\n    delete_response = client.delete(f\\\"/v3/events/{event_id}/\\\", headers=auth_headers())\\n\\n    assert delete_response.status_code == 200\\n    assert delete_response.json() == {\\\"deleted\\\": True}\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_publish_and_unpublish_event(client):\\n    publish = client.post(\\\"/v3/events/event_1/publish/\\\", headers=auth_headers())\\n\\n    assert publish.status_code == 200\\n    assert publish.json() == {\\\"published\\\": True}\\n\\n    unpublish = client.post(\\\"/v3/events/event_1/unpublish/\\\", headers=auth_headers())\\n\\n    assert unpublish.status_code == 200\\n    assert unpublish.json() == {\\\"unpublished\\\": True}\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_list_events_by_org_with_pagination(client):\\n    response = client.get(\\\"/v3/organizations/org_1/events/?page_size=2&page=1\\\", headers=auth_headers())\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 200\\n    body = response.json()\\n    assert body[\\\"pagination\\\"][\\\"page_size\\\"] == 2\\n    assert len(body[\\\"events\\\"]) <= 2\\nEOF\",\"yield_time_ms\":1000}",
    "call_id": "call_gHXmz8ctHwhLmRchm4v5ZOXa"
  }
}

\\\"utc\\\": \\\"2030-01-01T10:00:00Z\\\"},\\n            \\\"end\\\": {\\\"timezone\\\": \\\"UTC\\\", \\\"utc\\\": \\\"2030-01-01T12:00:00Z\\\"},\\n            \\\"currency\\\": \\\"USD\\\",\\n            \\\"venue_id\\\": \\\"venue_1\\\",\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_reject_event_with_venue_and_online_flag(client):\\n    payload = _base_event_payload()\\n    payload[\\\"event\\\"][\\\"online_event\\\"] = True\\n\\n    response = client.post(\\\"/v3/organizations/org_1/events/\\\", headers=auth_headers(), json=payload)\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 400\\n    body = response.json()\\n    assert body[\\\"error\\\"] == \\\"VENUE_AND_ONLINE\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_create_and_delete_draft_event(client):\\n    response = client.post(\\\"/v3/organizations/org_1/events/\\\", headers=auth_headers(), json=_base_event_payload())\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 200\\n    event_id = response.json()[\\\"id\\\"]\\n\\n    delete_response = client.delete(f\\\"/v3/events/{event_id}/\\\", headers=auth_headers())\\n\\n    assert delete_response.status_code == 200\\n    assert delete_response.json() == {\\\"deleted\\\": True}\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_publish_and_unpublish_event(client):\\n    publish = client.post(\\\"/v3/events/event_1/publish/\\\", headers=auth_headers())\\n\\n    assert publish.status_code == 200\\n    assert publish.json() == {\\\"published\\\": True}\\n\\n    unpublish = client.post(\\\"/v3/events/event_1/unpublish/\\\", headers=auth_headers())\\n\\n    assert unpublish.status_code == 200\\n    assert unpublish.json() == {\\\"unpublished\\\": True}\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_list_events_by_org_with_pagination(client):\\n    response = client.get(\\\"/v3/organizations/org_1/events/?page_size=2&page=1\\\", headers=auth_headers())\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 200\\n    body = response.json()\\n    assert body[\\\"pagination\\\"][\\\"page_size\\\"] == 2\\n    assert len(body[\\\"events\\\"]) <= 2\\nEOF\",\"yield_time_ms\":1000}",
    "call_id": "call_gHXmz8ctHwhLmRchm4v5ZOXa"
  }
}
```

    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat <<'EOF' > mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_mock_admin.py\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_return_dashboard_snapshot_with_seeded_ids(client):\\n    response = client.get(\\\"/mock/dashboard\\\")\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 200\\n    body = response.json()\\n    assert \\\"event_1\\\" in body[\\\"events\\\"]\\n    assert \\\"series_1\\\" in body[\\\"events\\\"]\\n    assert \\\"venue_1\\\" in body[\\\"venues\\\"]\\n    assert \\\"order_1\\\" in body[\\\"orders\\\"]\\n    assert \\\"attendee_1\\\" in body[\\\"attendees\\\"]\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_update_runtime_config(client):\\n    response = client.post(\\n        \\\"/mock/config\\\",\\n        json={\\n            \\\"transitionDelayMs\\\": 1234,\\n            \\\"generalRateLimit\\\": 10,\\n            \\\"dailyRateLimit\\\": 20,\\n            \\\"eventActionRateLimit\\\": 3,\\n            \\\"failureRates\\\": {\\\"order\\\": 0.1, \\\"refund\\\": 0.2, \\\"webhook\\\": 0.0},\\n        },\\n    )\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 200\\n    config = response.json()[\\\"config\\\"]\\n    assert config[\\\"transitionDelayMs\\\"] == 1234\\n    assert config[\\\"generalRateLimit\\\"] == 10\\n    assert config[\\\"dailyRateLimit\\\"] == 20\\n    assert config[\\\"eventActionRateLimit\\\"] == 3\\n    assert config[\\\"failureRates\\\"][\\\"order\\\"] == 0.1\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_reset_mock_state(client):\\n    response = client.post(\\\"/mock/reset\\\")\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 200\\n    body = response.json()\\n    assert body[\\\"message\\\"] == \\\"Mock state cleared.\\\"\\n    assert \\\"defaultConfig\\\" in body\\nEOF\",\"yield_time_ms\":1000}",
    "call_id": "call_fWPFG0wkLVPf3kv43QFnJ5wu"
  }
}

    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat <<'EOF' > mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_orders_attendees.py\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom tests.conftest import auth_headers\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_list_orders_by_event_with_refund_filter(client):\\n    response = client.get(\\n        \\\"/v3/events/event_1/orders/?refund_request_statuses=completed\\\",\\n        headers=auth_headers(),\\n    )\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 200\\n    body = response.json()\\n    assert body[\\\"orders\\\"][0][\\\"id\\\"] == \\\"order_1\\\"\\n    assert body[\\\"orders\\\"][0][\\\"refund_request\\\"][\\\"status\\\"] == \\\"completed\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef test_should_list_attendees_by_event_with_status_filter(client):\\n    response = client.get(\\n        \\\"/v3/events/event_1/attendees/?status=attending\\\",\\n        headers=auth_headers(),\\n    )\\n\\n    assert response.status_code == 200\\n    body = response.json()\\n    assert body[\\\"attendees\\\"][0][\\\"id\\\"] == \\\"attendee_1\\\"\\n    assert body[\\\"attendees\\\"][0][\\\"status\\\"] == \\\"attending\\\"\\nEOF\",\"yield_time_ms\":1000}",
    "call_id": "call_7usVFYbICDHYEHXyDGuyI8i4"
  }
}
```

    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_oZQSXNk78OB7tl8qzP54J1VU",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: mock-eventbrite-api/tests/conftest.py\n@@\n-import httpx\n-import pytest\n-\n-from app.main import create_app\n+import sys\n+from pathlib import Path\n+\n+import httpx\n+import pytest\n+\n+ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]\n+if str(ROOT) not in sys.path:\n+    sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))\n+\n+from app.main import create_app\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```

## Entry 236

**Role/Type:** response_item

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T04:18:16.981Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_KuY22t8Oe7qou6BO8NT0EoYg",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: mock-eventbrite-api/tests/conftest.py\n@@\n-import httpx\n-import pytest\n+import pytest\n+from fastapi.testclient import TestClient\n@@\n-    transport = httpx.ASGITransport(app=app, lifespan=\"on\")\n-    with httpx.Client(transport=transport, base_url=\"http://test\") as client:\n+    with TestClient(app) as client:\n         reset = client.post(\"/mock/reset\")\n         assert reset.status_code == 200\n         yield client\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```

    "name": "apply_patch",

  }
}

## Entry 266

**Role/Type:** event_msg

**Content:**

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T04:18:58.898Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Diagnosing duplicate seed data from persistent in-memory DB**\n\nThe in-memory SQLite engine persists across tests due to static pooling, causing startup seeding to duplicate data on each TestClient. To fix this, I need to reset the database before or immediately after app creation but before the TestClient runs startup to prevent double seeding."
  }
}
```

    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"ts=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S); ts_human=$(date +%Y-%m-%d\\\\ %H:%M:%S); cat <<EOF > /home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking/test_result_folder/mock_eventbrite_api_${ts}.md\\n# Test Result — Mock Eventbrite API\\n\\n- Timestamp: ${ts_human}\\n- Spec: N/A (mock-eventbrite-api)\\n- Module(s): mock-eventbrite-api\\n- Commit/Working State: local\\n\\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\\n- Total planned: 13\\n- Implemented: 13\\n- Passed: 13\\n- Failed: 0\\n- Skipped: 0\\n\\n## Failures Found\\n1. Initial run failed due to reserved SQLAlchemy attribute `metadata` on SeatMap.\\n   - Error: `InvalidRequestError: Attribute name 'metadata' is reserved`\\n   - Root cause: SQLAlchemy 2.0 reserves `metadata` as a declarative attribute.\\n   - Fix applied: Renamed model attribute to `metadata_json` and mapped column name to `metadata`.\\n2. Initial run failed due to duplicate seed inserts between tests.\\n   - Error: `sqlite3.IntegrityError: UNIQUE constraint failed: attendees.id`\\n   - Root cause: startup seeding ran on a shared in-memory DB across tests.\\n   - Fix applied: reset DB before each TestClient startup.\\n\\n## Code/Test Changes Applied\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/app/models/seatmap.py — renamed `metadata` attribute to `metadata_json` with column mapping\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/app/api/routes/seatmaps.py — map response from `metadata_json`\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/app/services/seed.py — seed using `metadata_json`\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/tests/conftest.py — add sys.path setup, reset DB per test, switch to TestClient\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_auth.py — auth + rate limit headers tests\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_events.py — event create/publish/list tests\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_mock_admin.py — admin endpoints tests\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_orders_attendees.py — order/attendee list filters tests\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/pyproject.toml — add pytest and requests\\n\\n## Final Status\\n- PASS\\n- Notes: FastAPI emits deprecation warnings for `on_event` usage.\\nEOF\",\"yield_time_ms\":1000}",
    "call_id": "call_i1QuQvPvhoOoWRitiAamUX6n"

}

    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"ts=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S); ts_human=$(date +%Y-%m-%d\\\\ %H:%M:%S); cat <<EOF > /home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking/test_result_folder/mock_eventbrite_api_${ts}.md\\n# Test Result — Mock Eventbrite API\\n\\n- Timestamp: ${ts_human}\\n- Spec: N/A (mock-eventbrite-api)\\n- Module(s): mock-eventbrite-api\\n- Commit/Working State: local\\n\\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\\n- Total planned: 13\\n- Implemented: 13\\n- Passed: 13\\n- Failed: 0\\n- Skipped: 0\\n\\n## Failures Found\\n1. Initial run failed due to reserved SQLAlchemy attribute named metadata on SeatMap.\\n   - Error: InvalidRequestError: Attribute name 'metadata' is reserved\\n   - Root cause: SQLAlchemy 2.0 reserves metadata as a declarative attribute.\\n   - Fix applied: Renamed model attribute to metadata_json and mapped column name to metadata.\\n2. Initial run failed due to duplicate seed inserts between tests.\\n   - Error: sqlite3.IntegrityError: UNIQUE constraint failed: attendees.id\\n   - Root cause: startup seeding ran on a shared in-memory DB across tests.\\n   - Fix applied: reset DB before each TestClient startup.\\n\\n## Code/Test Changes Applied\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/app/models/seatmap.py — renamed metadata attribute to metadata_json with column mapping\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/app/api/routes/seatmaps.py — map response from metadata_json\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/app/services/seed.py — seed using metadata_json\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/tests/conftest.py — add sys.path setup, reset DB per test, switch to TestClient\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_auth.py — auth + rate limit headers tests\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_events.py — event create/publish/list tests\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_mock_admin.py — admin endpoints tests\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_orders_attendees.py — order/attendee list filters tests\\n- mock-eventbrite-api/pyproject.toml — add pytest and requests\\n\\n## Final Status\\n- PASS\\n- Notes: FastAPI emits deprecation warnings for on_event usage.\\nEOF\",\"yield_time_ms\":1000}",
    "call_id": "call_xtsBh2z6cWvpzG1lAtUjHYJp"
  }
}

{

  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "Summary:\n- Added a pytest suite for the mock Eventbrite FastAPI app (auth, events, orders/attendees, and mock-admin endpoints).\n- Fixed a SQLAlchemy reserved attribute issue in `SeatMap` by renaming the model attribute to `metadata_json`.\n- Stabilized test setup by resetting the in-memory DB before each TestClient startup.\n\nTests (run outside sandbox):\n- `cd /home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking/mock-eventbrite-api && uv run pytest`\n- Result: 13 passed (with FastAPI `on_event` deprecation warnings)\n\nFiles changed:\n- `mock-eventbrite-api/app/models/seatmap.py`\n- `mock-eventbrite-api/app/api/routes/seatmaps.py`\n- `mock-eventbrite-api/app/services/seed.py`\n- `mock-eventbrite-api/tests/conftest.py`\n- `mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_auth.py`\n- `mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_events.py`\n- `mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_mock_admin.py`\n- `mock-eventbrite-api/tests/test_orders_attendees.py`\n- `mock-eventbrite-api/pyproject.toml`\n\nTest log:\n- `test_result_folder/mock_eventbrite_api_20260303_094951.md`\n\nIf you want, I can fold the FastAPI `on_event` deprecation warning into a small lifespan refactor.",
    "phase": null
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T04:20:37.713Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cb1ed-30cf-7ff1-ad30-993f8cc6cfc3",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-03",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "auto",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. {

    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: mock-eventbrite-api/app/main.py\n@@\n-from fastapi import FastAPI\n+from contextlib import asynccontextmanager\n+\n+from fastapi import FastAPI\n@@\n-def create_app() -> FastAPI:\n-    app = FastAPI(title=settings.app_name)\n+@asynccontextmanager\n+async def _lifespan(_: FastAPI):\n+    init_db()\n+    from app.database import SessionLocal\n+    from app.services.seed import seed_data\n+\n+    session = SessionLocal()\n+    try:\n+        seed_data(session)\n+    finally:\n+        session.close()\n+    yield\n+\n+\n+def create_app() -> FastAPI:\n+    app = FastAPI(title=settings.app_name, lifespan=_lifespan)\n     app.add_exception_handler(ApiError, api_error_handler)\n     app.add_middleware(RateLimitMiddleware)\n@@\n-    @app.on_event(\"startup\")\n-    def _startup():\n-        init_db()\n-        from app.database import SessionLocal\n-        from app.services.seed import seed_data\n-\n-        session = SessionLocal()\n-        try:\n-            seed_data(session)\n-        finally:\n-            session.close()\n-\n     return app\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```

    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: mock-eventbrite-api/app/main.py\n@@\n-from fastapi import FastAPI\n+from contextlib import asynccontextmanager\n+\n+from fastapi import FastAPI\n@@\n-def create_app() -> FastAPI:\n-    app = FastAPI(title=settings.app_name)\n+@asynccontextmanager\n+async def _lifespan(_: FastAPI):\n+    init_db()\n+    from app.database import SessionLocal\n+    from app.services.seed import seed_data\n+\n+    session = SessionLocal()\n+    try:\n+        seed_data(session)\n+    finally:\n+        session.close()\n+    yield\n+\n+\n+def create_app() -> FastAPI:\n+    app = FastAPI(title=settings.app_name, lifespan=_lifespan)\n     app.add_exception_handler(ApiError, api_error_handler)\n     app.add_middleware(RateLimitMiddleware)\n@@\n-    @app.on_event(\"startup\")\n-    def _startup():\n-        init_db()\n-        from app.database import SessionLocal\n-        from app.services.seed import seed_data\n-\n-        session = SessionLocal()\n-        try:\n-            seed_data(session)\n-        finally:\n-            session.close()\n-\n     return app\n*** End Patch"
  }
}

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T04:21:38.935Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cb1ee-1ff5-79e3-b2f9-8660aeb5b836",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-03",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "auto",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`.     "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn.
